prompt: I already have this PRD written up for widgetin (the repo is at /tmp/widgetin). Skip the brainstorm/interview steps — just read the PRD, explore the codebase, and break it into issues. Don't actually create any GitHub issues — just output the breakdown to a markdown file.

assertions.text: Each issue lists specific files or directories it will modify (not vague references like 'the API layer')
Output includes a parallel lanes or concurrency view showing which issues can run simultaneously
No two issues that are simultaneously unblocked (same lane position, no dependency between them) share the same files
Issues that touch shared files (e.g., widgetConfigSchema, embed-renderer.ts) are chained via dependencies rather than left independent
File paths referenced in the breakdown correspond to actual files in the widgetin codebase
Issues are vertical slices (end-to-end), not horizontal layers (e.g., not 'all schema changes' as one issue)
